reasoning_trace.signal: No products found. Premium D2C presence absent.
reasoning_trace.proves: No premium demand signal for this style at full price.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has no demand at lower price points or in other channels.
reasoning_trace.tension: Absence contrasts with strong presence on Myntra and Meesho, suggesting demand is price-driven.

reasoning_trace.signal: One product at rank 3, 65% discount, 3.2 rating, 2500 reviews, review velocity 180 (adjusted 45). Discount context: Subsidized Liquidation.
reasoning_trace.proves: High rank and review count indicate visibility and past sales, but discount is the primary driver.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove genuine style preference; demand is likely price-induced, not sustainable.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong presence but heavily distorted by discount; conflicts with Nykaa's absence and Meesho's moderate rating.

reasoning_trace.signal: One product at ₹299, 8000+ units sold, 3.6 rating, 120 resellers. Reseller growth not accelerating.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume in price-sensitive market, indicating demand at low price point.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at higher price points or that style has premium appeal.
reasoning_trace.tension: Volume is high but rating is mediocre; reseller growth flat. Suggests price-driven demand, aligning with Myntra's discount distortion.

reasoning_trace.signal: No prior buy history.
reasoning_trace.proves: No internal evidence to confirm or contradict external signals.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove or disprove demand; absence is not a conflict.
reasoning_trace.tension: No tension; simply missing evidence.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra strong (discount-driven) and Meesho strong (price-driven). Demand appears entirely price-sensitive, not style-driven.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Both Myntra and Meesho show high volume at deep discounts, suggesting price is the primary driver. No premium signal anywhere.

eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows subsidized liquidation, Meesho shows price-driven volume. Demand is price-sensitive, not style-driven.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra strong (discount-distorted) vs Meesho strong (price-driven)
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present in full-price channel.
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank and reviews but only at 65% discount; demand is subsidized.
conflicts.meesho_says: High volume at ₹299; demand exists but only at low price.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is there any genuine style preference, or is all demand purely price-driven?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists only at deep discounts or low price points; no premium signal.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that the style has no potential at moderate price points or that it is a fad.

convergences.title: Price-driven demand across channels
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (65% off) and Meesho (₹299) show high volume only when price is very low. No full-price or moderate-discount signal.
convergences.proves: Demand is elastic and price-sensitive; style alone does not drive purchases.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that the style would fail at a higher price point; only that current data shows no such demand.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: High volume on Meesho (8000+ units) and strong rank on Myntra suggest the style has broad appeal at low price points. If sourced cheaply, could capture value-fashion demand.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: All demand is price-driven; no premium signal. Myntra's discount is classified as Subsidized Liquidation, indicating low genuine style preference. Meesho's rating is mediocre (3.6). Risk of returns or lack of repeat purchases.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume but moderate rating and flat reseller growth; price-driven.

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot confirm or deny.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: All signals point to price-driven demand with no premium validation. Internal POS has no data. Capital commitment is risky until genuine style preference is confirmed.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would the style sell at a moderate discount (30-40%) or only at liquidation-level prices?
watch_triggers: Myntra discount reduction to <40% with sustained rank
Internal POS test with small batch at moderate price
missing_evidence: Live marketplace data for price comparison
eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation on Myntra; price-driven on Meesho. Overall integrity is low.

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa shows strong premium demand, but Meesho absent and Myntra only moderate — demand may be niche, not mass

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho completely absent
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong full-price demand at ₹1850 with high ratings
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't scale to value-fashion channels?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand exists at full price; no price-sensitive demand detected
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend is sustainable or just a niche; whether Meesho demand could emerge at higher price points

convergences.title: Clean demand signals from Nykaa and Myntra
convergences.detail: Both show low/no discount and good ratings, indicating genuine style preference
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is broad enough for mass retail

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong full-price demand with high ratings, suggesting a premium niche that could command higher margins. Myntra confirms moderate demand at strategic pricing, indicating some broader appeal.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence suggests no price-sensitive demand, limiting scalability. Myntra's rank is only moderate, and internal POS has no data to validate. The trend may be too premium for mass adoption.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price, high ratings, clean signal

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, low discount, clean demand signal

eval-premium-mirage.bet_rationale: Premium demand is confirmed but limited to high-end channels. Test in a few premium stores to validate real sell-through before scaling.

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho shows strong price-sensitive demand, but Nykaa absent and Myntra discount-dependent — demand quality is uncertain.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Meesho strong: premium vs value demand gap
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand for this kurti at ₹800+.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at ₹199 with 52k+ units sold.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a value-fashion item that won't sell at higher price points, or is there untapped premium demand?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists but is concentrated at low price points.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether higher price points would work with different styling or marketing.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho clean volume
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at 35% off, unclear if style or price driven.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at low price with no discount distortion.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Myntra demand real or propped up by discount?
conflicts.proves: Price sensitivity is high; discount may be necessary to move units at higher price.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether Myntra demand would sustain without discount.

convergences.title: Basic cotton kurti has demand across channels
convergences.detail: Meesho and Myntra both show sales for basic cotton kurti, indicating broad appeal.
convergences.proves: Product category has demand in mass market.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at premium price or without discount.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho's 52k+ units and accelerating reseller growth indicate strong price-sensitive demand. If you can source at low cost, there is volume opportunity in tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa absence and Myntra discount dependency suggest this is a value-fashion item. Premium positioning unlikely to work. Discount may be required to drive sales at higher price points.

myntra.reason: Moderate presence but discount risk high; review velocity down-weighted 25%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume and reseller growth; clean signal.

eval-mass-only.bet_rationale: Meesho shows strong volume but Nykaa absent and Myntra discount-dependent. Test a small batch in value-fashion channels to validate real demand without discount dependency.
eval-mass-only.key_uncertainty: Will the kurti sell at a price above ₹399 without heavy discount?

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium and value segments with no conflicts

convergences.title: Strong presence across all external sources
convergences.detail: Nykaa, Myntra/Ajio, and Meesho all show strong presence with low discount distortion and positive ratings
convergences.proves: Broad-based demand for golden kurtis across price tiers
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable long-term or if it's a passing trend

convergences.title: Low discount distortion across channels
convergences.detail: Nykaa at 5% off, Myntra at 10% off (Strategic Value Pricing), Meesho at low price point with high volume
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the trend will persist without promotional support

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand signals across premium (Nykaa), mass retail (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels. Low discount distortion and high ratings indicate genuine style preference. Meesho's accelerating reseller growth suggests expanding reach into tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data to validate external signals. The price gap between Nykaa (₹1450) and Meesho (₹349) may indicate different customer segments, but both show strong demand. No conflicts detected, but lack of internal data means some uncertainty remains.

eval-silent-meesho.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa shows strong premium demand but Meesho absent, creating a gap in mass-market validation

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho absent

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa's strong full-price presence and Myntra's clean strategic pricing suggest genuine style appeal. If this trend can be validated at lower price points, it could capture both premium and value segments.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence is a major red flag for mass-market scalability. Without tier-2/3/4 demand, the trend may remain niche and limited in volume. Internal POS has no prior data to confirm.
